mnemonics.title: PROLOG Meaning
mnemonics.content: PROLOG = PROgramming in LOGic. Remember: 'PRO' means 'for/forward' and 'LOG' means 'logic' - programming using logic forward!

mnemonics.title: Variables vs Constants
mnemonics.content: UPPERCASE = Variables (X, Y, Who), lowercase = constants (john, mary, likes). Think: 'V' in Variable looks like uppercase; 'c' in constant looks like lowercase!

mnemonics.title: List Decomposition
mnemonics.content: [H|T] - Remember: H is for HEAD (first), T is for TAIL (rest). Like T follows H in the alphabet!

acronyms.usage: The name reflects Prolog's core philosophy: you define logical relationships, and the system derives conclusions.
[H|T] notation splits lists into first element (Head) and remaining elements (Tail).

visualTips.visualization: Think of Facts as database records, Rules as if-then statements, and Queries as questions with a question mark (?) - just like a database query!
Imagine a tree with branches. When one branch fails, you 'back up' to try another branch. Like exploring a maze - if you hit a dead end, return to the last choice point!
Think of unification as pattern matching - like fitting puzzle pieces together. If pieces fit, variables get bound to specific values!
Draw a scissors icon (!) cutting off branches of the search tree. The cut 'prunes' alternative paths, committing to the current solution path.

analogies.analogy: Imperative languages are like a recipe with step-by-step instructions. Prolog is like a list of facts and rules - like a detective's case file where you ask questions and let the system figure out the answers!
A Prolog knowledge base is like a giant web of true statements (like Wikipedia facts). The query is like asking a question to this database, and Prolog finds all matching entries!
Variables in Prolog are like wildcards or empty placeholders - like empty slots in a form waiting to be filled with specific information!
Backtracking is like a smart search in a library. If one book doesn't have the answer, you go back and try another book until you find all possible answers!
